    "Existing equity MF look-through shows a quality-oriented, large-cap-tilted portfolio: Mirae Asset Large Cap at P/E 25.92 and P/B 3.7, Axis Large Cap at P/E 27.71 and P/B 4.18, and Parag Parikh Flexi Cap at P/E 18.45 and P/B 3.05 — the PPFCF multiple is notably lower, consistent with its international equity sleeve (US tech at compressed multiples) and value-aware mandate.",

    "Stallion Asset Core Fund is a quality-concentrated PMS with a documented bias toward high-ROCE, low-leverage businesses — its published strategy emphasises businesses with ROCE above 20%, low debt, and high earnings visibility. This is directionally consistent with the existing MF portfolio's quality orientation, creating meaningful strategy overlap rather than genuine diversification.",

The net effect on the portfolio's listed-equity quality/valuation profile is marginal: one quality large-cap vehicle is partially replaced by another quality-concentrated vehicle, with no material shift in underlying sector or factor exposure.",

  "reasoning_paragraph": "The existing equity mutual fund portfolio presents a coherent quality-and-large-cap orientation: Mirae Asset Large Cap (P/E 25.92, P/B 3.7), Axis Large Cap (P/E 27.71, P/B 4.18), and Parag Parikh Flexi Cap (P/E 18.45, P/B 3.05) collectively imply a blended fund-level P/E in the 24–24.5x range, a moderate premium to broad market that is consistent with quality-factor positioning rather than valuation excess. Parag Parikh's lower multiple reflects its international equity sleeve and value-aware mandate, providing the only meaningful valuation differentiation within the disclosed universe. The proposed Stallion Asset Core Fund PMS is a quality-concentrated strategy with a stated preference for high-ROCE, low-leverage businesses — a profile that is directionally redundant with the existing large-cap quality MF cluster rather than additive. The Rs 50 L partial trim of Mirae Asset Large Cap to fund the PMS ticket does not materially alter the portfolio's aggregate listed-equity quality or valuation profile; it substitutes one quality vehicle for another at a higher fee and concentration level. The Kotak Emerging Equity Fund (10.5% of AUM) remains outside the look-through universe, which limits the completeness of this aggregate assessment and is the primary driver of the reduced confidence score. The risk level is assessed as moderate: not from valuation excess or earnings quality concerns in the disclosed holdings, but from the structural incompleteness of the look-through and the concentration shift that a quality-concentrated PMS introduces into an already quality-tilted equity book.",
